executive_summary.priority_finding: Non-atomic writes to critical JSON files (fleet.json, state.json) and partial conflict resolution in merge script represent P0 data corruption risks

todo_comments.notes: No actionable TODO comments found. References to 'TODO' exist only in tooling that searches for TODOs (meta-references).

fixme_comments.notes: No FIXME comments found. Meta-references exist in quality-checking mechanisms.

incomplete_functions.notes: All functions are implemented. Patterns like 'pass' occur only in exception handlers, not as stubs. Test smoke agent contains intentional mock but is implemented.

critical_findings.issue: Partial conflict resolution - sed failure breaks loop but previous files already git-added
critical_findings.impact: Corrupted merge commit with mix of resolved and unresolved conflicts
critical_findings.fix: Track files to add in array; only git add + commit after ALL files resolve successfully

critical_findings.issue: state.json teardown write is non-atomic (direct overwrite)
critical_findings.fix: Use tmp+rename pattern like mark_run_blocked does

critical_findings.issue: fleet.json write is non-atomic despite write_json_atomic being available
critical_findings.impact: Monitor reads fleet.json every 5s; truncated file crashes monitor or shows wrong data
critical_findings.fix: Use write_json_atomic() which is already defined at L643

critical_findings.impact: Monitor reads this for nested commander progress; corruption fails display silently

critical_findings.issue: Orphan recovery mv lacks error logging (stderr suppressed with 2>/dev/null)
critical_findings.impact: Failed mv leaves task in claimed/ forever - silent task loss
critical_findings.fix: Replace 2>/dev/null with log file; add || branch for error message

critical_findings.issue: git checkout main/master fallback kills script with no message if neither exists
critical_findings.impact: All merge work lost with cryptic error
critical_findings.fix: Add explicit error message before exit 1

critical_findings.issue: git pull --ff-only failure suppressed with || true
critical_findings.impact: Merge proceeds on stale base causing spurious conflicts
critical_findings.fix: At minimum log warning about stale base

critical_findings.issue: PID capture relies on fixed sleep 5 with no validation
critical_findings.impact: On slow systems captures wrong PID; teardown misses agents
critical_findings.fix: Retry PID capture in loop until leaf PID matches known agent binary

critical_findings.issue: PID file write is non-atomic (direct echo >)
critical_findings.impact: Monitor/teardown reads empty PID during truncation
Command failures in main loop continue silently
critical_findings.fix: Add set -e or wrap critical operations with explicit error handling

error_handling_gaps.theme: Inconsistent application of atomic write pattern (tmp+mv) across JSON files; merge script conflict resolution is fragile

findings.issue: Forces TOTAL_TASKS=1 when no tasks found to avoid divide-by-zero
findings.fix: Explicit zero-task handling or validation state

findings.issue: Falls back to 50 squad leads / 250 workers if telemetry missing
findings.impact: Masks broken telemetry and shows false completeness
findings.fix: Read from swarm-state.json/fleet.json or fail loudly

findings.impact: Demo-only; medium risk if copied to real workflows

findings.issue: Fake test-suite data and hardcoded defaults
findings.fix: Real telemetry/config inputs if meant to reflect actual runs

immediate_actions: Fix P0 non-atomic writes to fleet.json, state.json, swarm-state.json using existing write_json_atomic helper
Fix P0 merge script partial conflict resolution - track all files before git add/commit
Add error logging to orphan recovery mv operation
Fix git checkout fallback to provide clear error message
follow_up_actions: Implement PID capture retry loop with validation
Add set -e to stampede-monitor.sh or explicit error handling
Remove hardcoded metaswarm targets fallback or make it fail-loud
Add input validation to find_leaf_pid and get_worker_model functions
code_quality_notes: Core implementation is solid with no TODO/FIXME debt
Error handling pattern (atomic writes) exists but inconsistently applied
Placeholder code is minimal and mostly confined to demo/visualization tooling

quality_score.notes: Excellent code completeness with no technical debt (TODO/FIXME/stubs). Error handling has critical atomicity gaps that must be addressed. Minimal placeholder code.
